downstream_consumers: Dashboard Intelligence tab (dispatch-009 data API server serves these files)

risks.title: No layer/capability field in index.json
risks.mitigation: Use element.type as the color/grouping dimension for treemap and scatter. Dispatch context confirms this is acceptable for the first iteration.

risks.title: umap-js not available — PCA fallback produces linear projection
risks.mitigation: PCA on 768-dim vectors still produces meaningful 2D structure. Dispatch allows 'fallback to PCA if umap unavailable'. Note in output file metadata.

risks.title: Complexity data in summary.json not keyed by codeRefId
risks.mitigation: Join on element name + file path. Some elements may have zero complexity if join misses — treat as complexity=1 (baseline).

risks.title: Large files (~5MB index, ~24MB graph) may be slow to parse
risks.mitigation: Parse once, compute all 5 datasets in a single Node.js script pass. No streaming required.

decision_records.decision: Implement all 5 datasets in a single generator script: scripts/generate-intelligence.js
decision_records.rationale: Single pass over large JSON files. Reusable after populate runs. No dependency on the dashboard server (dispatch-009).

decision_records.decision: Use element.type as the color/layer dimension for treemap and scatter
decision_records.rationale: No layer field in current index. Type (function, class, method, interface, type) provides meaningful grouping visible in the viz.

decision_records.decision: PCA implemented inline using covariance matrix decomposition
decision_records.rationale: Avoids npm dependency for umap-js. Pure math produces 2D projection from 768-dim vectors. Fully deterministic for reproducible output.

decision_records.decision: Sankey aggregates at directory level (top-level src/ subdirectory)
decision_records.rationale: Element-to-element sankey would have 2431 nodes — too large for D3 rendering. Directory-level aggregation produces a readable flow diagram.
